   "source": [
    "# Fitting: Basics\n",
    "\n",
    "**Goal**: Learn how to fit models to GW data using Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) and Generalized Least Squares (GLS).\n",
    "\n",
    "GWexpy uses **iminuit** as its primary optimization engine and provides specialized cost functions for spectral data with correlations."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "68f2b11f",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.008548,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:22.936083+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:22.927535+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Simple OLS Fit\n",
    "\n",
    "We start with a simple power-law fit using diagonal errors (OLS)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "2cde139e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T02:40:22.981820Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T02:40:22.981578Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T02:40:35.928262Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T02:40:35.927565Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 12.964328,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:35.936061+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:22.971733+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Fitted a=2.00, b=9.73\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.fitting import fit_series\n",
    "\n",
    "# Generate synthetic data\n",
    "freqs = np.linspace(10, 100, 50)\n",
    "model = lambda f, a, b: a * f + b\n",
    "y_true = model(freqs, 2.0, 10.0)\n",
    "y_obs = y_true + np.random.normal(0, 2, size=len(freqs))\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.frequencyseries import FrequencySeries\n",
    "\n",
    "psd = FrequencySeries(y_obs, frequencies=freqs)\n",
    "\n",
    "res = fit_series(psd, model, p0={\"a\": 1.0, \"b\": 1.0})\n",
    "\n",
    "res.plot()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "print(f\"Fitted a={res.params['a']:.2f}, b={res.params['b']:.2f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c9eba214",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.019183,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:35.973621+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:35.954438+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Generalized Least Squares (GLS)\n",
    "\n",
    "When data points are correlated (e.g., in a PSD estimate with overlapping windows), we use the covariance matrix $\\Sigma$."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "e55baf8a",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.060708Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.060115Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.077648Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.076156Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.044179,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.078665+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.034486+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "<ValueView a=1.9989571764066376 b=9.726580910114837>\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "from iminuit import Minuit\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.fitting import GeneralizedLeastSquares\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create a dummy covariance matrix\n",
    "cov = np.eye(len(freqs)) * 4.0\n",
    "cov_inv = np.linalg.inv(cov)\n",
    "\n",
    "# 1. Define Cost Function\n",
    "cost = GeneralizedLeastSquares(freqs, y_obs, cov_inv, model, cov=cov)\n",
    "\n",
    "# 2. Initialize and Run Minuit\n",
    "m = Minuit(cost, a=1.0, b=1.0)\n",
    "m.migrad()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(m.values)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "90d01793",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.009245,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.098365+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.089120+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. High-level Integration\n",
    "\n",
    "In real analysis, we often estimate the covariance matrix from the data itself using bootstrap methods. GWexpy provides an integrated pipeline for this."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "2f742690",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.156004Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.155694Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T02:40:52.714834Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T02:40:52.714020Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 16.578931,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:52.716427+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:36.137496+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "from gwexpy.fitting.highlevel import fit_bootstrap_spectrum\n",
    "from gwexpy.noise.wave import colored\n",
    "\n",
    "# Generate 10 seconds of pink noise\n",
    "data = colored(duration=10, sample_rate=256, exponent=0.5, amplitude=1e-21)\n",
    "\n",
    "def power_law(f, A, alpha):\n",
    "    return A * f**alpha\n",
    "\n",
    "result = fit_bootstrap_spectrum(\n",
    "    data,\n",
    "    model_fn=power_law,\n",
    "    freq_range=(10, 100),\n",
    "    fftlength=1.0,\n",
    "    overlap=0.5,\n",
    "    initial_params={\"A\": 1e-21, \"alpha\": -0.5},\n",
    "    plot=True\n",
    ")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "fa32b61a",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.010122,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:52.738176+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T02:40:52.728054+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 4. Exercises\n",
    "\n",
    "1. **Parameter Bounds**: Modify the OLS fit to restrict `a > 0` using the `limits` argument in `fit_series`.\n",
    "2. **MCMC**: Enable MCMC in Section 3 by setting `run_mcmc=True` (requires `emcee`)."
   ]
  },
